questions.prompt:
An e-commerce company runs a MySQL database on Amazon RDS to store order and customer data. The database currently runs as a single-AZ deployment in us-east-1a. The business team has expressed concerns about potential downtime, as the application experienced a 45-minute outage last month during unplanned database maintenance.

Business Requirements:
- RPO (Recovery Point Objective): 0 seconds - no data loss acceptable
- RTO (Recovery Time Objective): Less than 2 minutes
- Minimize operational overhead
- Maintain high data durability
- Automatic failover without manual intervention
- Support for both planned and unplanned outages

Which solution provides the MOST appropriate high availability configuration?

options.text: Enable Multi-AZ deployment for the RDS instance, which creates a synchronous standby replica in a different Availability Zone with automatic failover
Create Read Replicas in two additional Availability Zones and configure the application to automatically promote a Read Replica to master in case of failure
Take automated snapshots every 5 minutes and configure Lambda to restore from the latest snapshot to a new RDS instance if failure is detected
Deploy the application across multiple regions with RDS instances in each region, using Route 53 health checks to redirect traffic to healthy regions

questions.explanation:
**Why Multi-AZ is the correct solution:**

**RDS Multi-AZ Architecture:**

```
в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
в”Ӯ         Application Tier                в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ    (Connects to Single Endpoint)        в”Ӯ
в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
               в”Ӯ DNS: mydb.abc123.rds.amazonaws.com
               вҶ“
в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
в”Ӯ              us-east-1a                  в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ   в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ            в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ  Primary RDS Instance  в”Ӯ            в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ    (Read/Write)        в”Ӯ            в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ            в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ              в”Ӯ Synchronous Replication  в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ              вҶ“                           в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ   в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ            в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ  EBS Volume (Primary)  в”Ӯ            в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ            в”Ӯ
в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
               вҶ“ Sync
в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
в”Ӯ              us-east-1b                  в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ   в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ            в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ  Standby RDS Instance  в”Ӯ            в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ    (No Read/Write)     в”Ӯ            в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ            в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ              в”Ӯ                           в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ   в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ            в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ  EBS Volume (Standby)  в”Ӯ            в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ            в”Ӯ
в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
```

**How Multi-AZ Works:**

1. **Synchronous Replication:**
   - Every write to primary is **synchronously** replicated to standby
   - Transaction only commits after standby acknowledges receipt
   - **RPO = 0 seconds** (no data loss) вң“

2. **Automatic Failover:**
   - AWS detects failure (primary instance, AZ, or storage)
   - Automatically promotes standby to primary
   - Updates DNS record (endpoint stays the same)
   - **RTO = 1-2 minutes typically** вң“

3. **Single Endpoint:**
   - Application uses same endpoint before and after failover
   - No application code changes needed
   - DNS TTL is 30 seconds

**Failover Triggers:**
- Primary DB instance failure
- Availability Zone failure
- Primary instance server type change
- Operating system patching
- Manual failover initiated (for testing)
- Primary DB instance storage failure
- Primary network failure

**Enabling Multi-AZ:**

```bash
# Enable Multi-AZ on existing instance
aws rds modify-db-instance \
  --db-instance-identifier mydb \
  --multi-az \
  --apply-immediately

# Create new Multi-AZ instance
aws rds create-db-instance \
  --db-instance-identifier mydb \
  --db-instance-class db.t3.large \
  --engine mysql \
  --master-username admin \
  --master-user-password MyPassword123 \
  --allocated-storage 100 \
  --multi-az \
  --backup-retention-period 7
```

**Benefits of Multi-AZ:**

вң… **Zero Data Loss (RPO = 0):** Synchronous replication
вң… **Fast Recovery (RTO = 1-2 min):** Automatic failover
вң… **No Manual Intervention:** Fully automated
вң… **Transparent to Application:** Same endpoint
вң… **Automatic Backups:** Taken from standby (no I/O impact)
вң… **Planned Maintenance:** Zero-downtime patching
вң… **High Durability:** Data in two AZs

**Performance Considerations:**
- ~5-10% write latency increase (due to sync replication)
- No read scalability (standby not accessible for reads)
- Doubles storage costs (data stored in two AZs)

**Why other options are WRONG:**

**Option B (Read Replicas):**

вқҢ **Asynchronous replication:**
- Data loss possible during failover
- **RPO > 0 seconds** (violates requirement)
- Replication lag can be seconds to minutes

вқҢ **Manual promotion:**
- Requires manual intervention or custom scripts
- Takes several minutes to promote
- Application must update endpoint
- **RTO > 2 minutes** (violates requirement)

вқҢ **Application changes required:**
- Must detect failure
- Must trigger promotion
- Must update connection string
- Complex error handling

**Read Replica Use Case (NOT HA):**
```
в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ  Async   в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
в”Ӯ   Primary   в”Ӯ ------вҶ’  в”Ӯ Read Replicaв”Ӯ
в”Ӯ (us-east-1a)в”Ӯ Replicationв”Ӯ(us-east-1b)в”Ӯ
в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ          в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
  Write traffic            Read traffic
```

**Option C (Snapshot Restoration):**

вқҢ **Data Loss:**
- 5-minute snapshot interval = up to 5 minutes of data loss
- **RPO = 5 minutes** (violates requirement)

вқҢ **Long Recovery Time:**
- Snapshot restore takes 10-30 minutes for 100 GB database
- Plus time for Lambda to detect failure
- **RTO = 10-30 minutes** (violates requirement)

вқҢ **New Instance:**
- Different endpoint (requires application update)
- DNS propagation delay
- Connection string changes

вқҢ **Cost:**
- Snapshot storage costs
- Lambda costs
- Custom monitoring solution
- High operational overhead

**Option D (Multi-Region):**

вқҢ **Overkill for requirements:**
- Disaster recovery solution, not HA
- Much more complex
- Significantly higher costs
- Cross-region replication latency

вқҢ **Costs:**
- Double infrastructure costs (two regions)
- Cross-region data transfer: $0.02/GB
- Route 53 health checks
- Operational complexity

вқҢ **When to use:**
- Disaster recovery (entire region failure)
- Global read scalability
- Data residency requirements
- RTO/RPO > Multi-AZ capabilities

**Multi-AZ vs Read Replicas vs Multi-Region:**

| Feature | Multi-AZ | Read Replica | Multi-Region |
|---------|----------|--------------|-------------|
| Purpose | HA | Read scaling | DR |
| Replication | Sync | Async | Async |
| RPO | 0 seconds | Seconds-minutes | Minutes |
| RTO | 1-2 minutes | 5-10 minutes | 10-30 minutes |
| Failover | Automatic | Manual | Manual |
| Endpoint | Same | Different | Different |
| Read traffic | No | Yes | Yes |
| Cost | ~2x | 1x per replica | 2x++ |
| Use case | HA in same region | Read scaling | DR across regions |

**Cost Comparison (db.t3.large, 100 GB):**

- **Single-AZ:** ~$125/month
- **Multi-AZ:** ~$250/month (2x storage + standby instance)
- **Read Replica:** ~$125/month per replica
- **Multi-Region:** ~$500/month (2 regions Г— 2 Г— $125)

**Testing Multi-AZ Failover:**

```bash
# Initiate failover for testing
aws rds reboot-db-instance \
  --db-instance-identifier mydb \
  --force-failover

# Monitor failover
aws rds describe-db-instances \
  --db-instance-identifier mydb \
  --query 'DBInstances[0].DBInstanceStatus'
```

**Best Practices:**
1. Enable Multi-AZ for production databases
2. Test failover regularly
3. Monitor RDS metrics (CPU, connections, lag)
4. Use Enhanced Monitoring for OS-level metrics
5. Configure CloudWatch alarms
6. Set appropriate backup retention (7-35 days)
7. Enable automated backups (required for Multi-AZ)

References:
- Multi-AZ Deployments: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonRDS/latest/UserGuide/Concepts.MultiAZ.html
- RDS High Availability: https://aws.amazon.com/rds/features/multi-az/
- RDS Failover: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonRDS/latest/UserGuide/Concepts.MultiAZSingleStandby.html
questions.incorrect: This solution either cannot meet the RPO requirement of zero data loss (Read Replicas use asynchronous replication, snapshots have gaps), takes too long to recover (RTO > 2 minutes for snapshot restore or multi-region failover), or requires manual intervention (promoting Read Replicas). Multi-AZ provides synchronous replication (RPO=0), automatic failover in 1-2 minutes (RTO<2 min), and requires no application changes.
questions.hint: Which RDS feature provides synchronous replication with automatic failover and zero data loss?
